data.content:
### Trigger Spot Interruption for Testing

Source: https://context7.com/runs-on/runs-on/llms.txt

Triggers a spot interruption event for specified EC2 instances, useful for testing resilience and interruption handling in your CI/CD pipelines. This command requires instance IDs as input.

```bash
make trigger-spot-interruption i-0abc123 i-0def456
```

--------------------------------

### Job Runs-On Label Examples

Source: https://github.com/runs-on/runs-on/blob/main/adrs/20250727-warm-pools.md

Illustrates how to specify a target pool for a job using the 'runs-on' directive with the 'pool=' label. This allows for deterministic assignment of jobs to specific warm pool instances.

```yaml
runs-on: runs-on/pool=cheap-x64
# or for more deterministic runner <-> job assignment
runs-on: runs-on=${{ github.run_id }}/pool=cheap-x64
```

--------------------------------

### Job Label System - Warm Pool Job

Source: https://context7.com/runs-on/runs-on/llms.txt

Configures a job to use a specific warm pool ('small-x64') defined in 'runs-on.yml'. This ensures fast startup times by leveraging pre-warmed instances.

```yaml
jobs:
  # Warm pool with fast startup
  pool-job:
    runs-on: runs-on,pool=small-x64,env=production
```

--------------------------------

### Warm Pool Configurations

Source: https://context7.com/runs-on/runs-on/llms.txt

Configures warm pools for fast runner startup. It defines schedules for 'hot' (running) and 'stopped' (warmed) instances based on time and day, allowing for efficient resource utilization.

```yaml
# .github/runs-on.yml

# Warm pools for fast startup (<10s for hot, ~20s for stopped)
pools:
  # Hot pool - instances stay running
  small-x64:
    env: production
    runner: fast-build
    timezone: "America/New_York"
    schedule:
      - name: business-hours
        match:
          day: ["monday", "tuesday", "wednesday", "thursday", "friday"]
          time: ["08:00", "18:00"]
        stopped: 0
        hot: 5
      - name: off-hours
        match:
          day: ["monday", "tuesday", "wednesday", "thursday", "friday"]
          time: ["18:00", "08:00"]
        stopped: 2
        hot: 1
      - name: weekends
        match:
          day: ["saturday", "sunday"]
        stopped: 2
        hot: 0

  # Stopped pool - instances warmed then stopped
  dev-pool:
    env: dev
    runner: fast-build
    timezone: "Europe/Paris"
    schedule:
      - name: default
        stopped: 3
        hot: 0
```

--------------------------------

### Runs On Pool Configuration YAML

Source: https://github.com/runs-on/runs-on/blob/main/adrs/20250727-warm-pools.md

Defines the structure for configuring runner specifications and warm pools within the .github-private/.github/runs-on.yml file. It specifies image, resources for runners, and pools with scheduling and environment details.

```yaml
runners:
  small-x64:
    image: ubuntu24-full-x64
    ram: 1
    family: [t3]
    volume: gp3:30gb:125mbps:3000iops

pools:
  small-x64:
    # If you have multiple RunsOn stacks, you can specify the environment this pool belongs to
    env: dev
    runner: small-x64
    timezone: "Europe/Paris"
    schedule:
      - name: default
        stopped: 2
        hot: 1
      - name: nights
        match:
          day: ["monday", "tuesday", "wednesday", "thursday", "friday", "saturday", "sunday"]
          time: ["22:00", "06:00"]
        stopped: 2
        hot: 0
      - name: weekends
        match:
          day: ["saturday", "sunday"]
        stopped: 1
        hot: 0
```

--------------------------------

### Job Label System - Multiple Extras Combined

Source: https://context7.com/runs-on/runs-on/llms.txt

Configures a job to utilize multiple extra features like S3 caching, EFS, tmpfs, and ECR cache simultaneously using the job label system. This enables comprehensive build and deployment environments.

```yaml
jobs:
  # Multiple extras combined
  full-featured:
    runs-on: runs-on,cpu=8,ram=16,extras=s3-cache+efs+tmpfs+ecr-cache,env=production
```

--------------------------------

### Job Label System - Secure Job with Private Networking

Source: https://context7.com/runs-on/runs-on/llms.txt

Configures a secure job that uses private networking, specified by 'private=true', along with custom CPU resources. This is suitable for tasks requiring isolated network access.

```yaml
jobs:
  # Private networking with static IP
  secure-job:
    runs-on: runs-on,cpu=2,private=true,env=production
```

--------------------------------

### Private Networking with Static IPs

Source: https://context7.com/runs-on/runs-on/llms.txt

Sets up a job to use private networking with a static IP address. This is useful for secure deployments to internal services.

```yaml
jobs:
  secure-deploy:
    runs-on: runs-on,runner=2cpu-linux-x64,private=true,env=production
    steps:
      - run: curl https://internal-api.company.com/deploy
```

--------------------------------

### Job Label System - Minimal Configuration

Source: https://context7.com/runs-on/runs-on/llms.txt

Demonstrates the minimal configuration for a job using the job label system, specifying only the environment as 'production'.

```yaml
# Available label parameters:
# - runs-on=<group>         # Deterministic grouping (use github.run_id)
# - runner=<name>           # Named runner from runs-on.yml
# - cpu=<count>             # CPU cores (1-96)
# - ram=<gb>                # Memory in GB (2-384)
# - family=<type>           # EC2 instance family (c7a, m7a, r7a, g4dn, etc.)
# - image=<name>            # AMI image name
# - volume=<spec>           # Storage: <size>:<type>:<throughput>:<iops>
# - pool=<name>             # Warm pool name
# - env=<environment>       # Environment filter (production, dev, staging)
# - private=<bool>          # Use private networking
# - extras=<features>       # Additional features (s3-cache, efs, tmpfs, ecr-cache)

jobs:
  # Minimal configuration
  minimal:
    runs-on: runs-on,env=production
```

--------------------------------

### Job Label System - Specific Instance Family

Source: https://context7.com/runs-on/runs-on/llms.txt

Configures a job to use a specific EC2 instance family ('c7a.4xlarge') via the job label system. This is useful for selecting optimized instance types for compute-intensive workloads.

```yaml
jobs:
  # Specific instance family
  compute-optimized:
    runs-on: runs-on,family=c7a.4xlarge,env=production
```

--------------------------------

### Job Label System - GPU Job with Specific Image

Source: https://context7.com/runs-on/runs-on/llms.txt

Sets up a GPU job specifying both the instance family ('g4dn.xlarge') and a particular AMI image ('ubuntu22-gpu-x64') using the job label system. This is ideal for machine learning tasks.

```yaml
jobs:
  # GPU with specific image
  gpu-job:
    runs-on: runs-on,family=g4dn.xlarge,image=ubuntu22-gpu-x64,env=production
```

--------------------------------

### GitHub Actions Workflow with Private Networking

Source: https://context7.com/runs-on/runs-on/llms.txt

Sets up a GitHub Actions job to run within a private network, forcing all egress traffic through a NAT gateway with a static IP. This enhances security by preventing direct public internet access.

```yaml
jobs:
  secure-build:
    runs-on: runs-on,cpu=4,private=true,env=production
    steps:
      - run: |
          # No public IP, egress via NAT gateway with static IP
          curl https://api.github.com
```

--------------------------------

### GitHub Actions Workflow with ECR Docker Layer Caching

Source: https://context7.com/runs-on/runs-on/llms.txt

Implements Docker layer caching using an Amazon ECR registry within a GitHub Actions workflow. This significantly accelerates Docker image builds by pulling and pushing cached layers.

```yaml
jobs:
  docker-build:
    runs-on: runs-on,cpu=4,extras=ecr-cache,env=production
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: docker/setup-buildx-action@v3
      - uses: docker/build-push-action@v4
        with:
          context: .
          push: true
          tags: ${{ env.RUNS_ON_ECR_CACHE }}:myapp:latest
          cache-from: type=registry,ref=${{ env.RUNS_ON_ECR_CACHE }}:myapp:buildcache
          cache-to: type=registry,ref=${{ env.RUNS_ON_ECR_CACHE }}:myapp:buildcache,mode=max
```

--------------------------------

### View CloudWatch EC2 Logs

Source: https://context7.com/runs-on/runs-on/llms.txt

Fetches and displays EC2 instance logs from CloudWatch. This command requires the AWS region, log group name, and options for output formatting. It continuously streams logs with a specified lookback time.

```bash
awslogs get --aws-region us-east-1 /aws/runs-on/runs-on-prod/ec2 -w -s 10m --timestamp
```

--------------------------------

### Job Label System - Custom CPU and Memory

Source: https://context7.com/runs-on/runs-on/llms.txt

Configures a job with custom CPU and memory specifications using the job label system. This allows fine-tuning runner resources for specific tasks.

```yaml
jobs:
  # Custom CPU and memory
  custom-specs:
    runs-on: runs-on,cpu=16,ram=64,env=production
```

--------------------------------

### View CloudWatch Application Logs

Source: https://context7.com/runs-on/runs-on/llms.txt

Fetches and displays application logs from CloudWatch. This command requires the AWS region, log group name, and options for output formatting. It continuously streams logs with a specified lookback time.

```bash
awslogs get --aws-region us-east-1 /aws/apprunner/RunsOnService-xxx/yyy/application -w -s 10m --timestamp
```

--------------------------------

### Stream Local Logs to CloudWatch

Source: https://context7.com/runs-on/runs-on/llms.txt

Streams local development logs to CloudWatch for real-time monitoring and dashboarding. This command should be run in a separate terminal alongside your development environment.

```bash
make dev-stream-logs
```

--------------------------------

### Job Label System - Custom Storage Configuration

Source: https://context7.com/runs-on/runs-on/llms.txt

Sets up a job with a custom storage configuration, including size, type, throughput, and IOPS, using the job label system. This provides flexibility for storage-intensive tasks.

```yaml
jobs:
  # Custom storage configuration
  large-storage:
    runs-on: runs-on,cpu=4,volume=200gb:gp3:1000mbps:5000iops,env=production
```

--------------------------------

### Access Prometheus Metrics Endpoint

Source: https://context7.com/runs-on/runs-on/llms.txt

Retrieves Prometheus metrics from the running stack. Requires authentication with a server password. The endpoint URL and credentials are necessary inputs.

```bash
curl -u admin:your-server-password https://your-stack-url.com/metrics
```

--------------------------------

### Job Label System - Named Runner Reference

Source: https://context7.com/runs-on/runs-on/llms.txt

Configures a job to use a named runner ('fast-build') from the 'runs-on.yml' file, along with the specified environment. This promotes reusability of runner configurations.

```yaml
jobs:
  # Named runner reference
  use-named:
    runs-on: runs-on,runner=fast-build,env=production
```

--------------------------------

### GitHub Actions Workflow Configuration (YAML)

Source: https://github.com/runs-on/runs-on/blob/main/README.md

Demonstrates how to switch from the default 'ubuntu-latest' runner to a RunsOn dynamic runner. This allows leveraging RunsOn's features by specifying a custom runner label. The input is a YAML workflow file, and the output is the modified workflow configuration.

```yaml
  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
```

```yaml
  runs-on: "runs-on=${{ github.run_id }}/runner=2cpu-linux-x64"
```

--------------------------------

### Custom AMI Images Configuration

Source: https://context7.com/runs-on/runs-on/llms.txt

Defines custom Amazon Machine Images (AMIs) for runners, including GPU-enabled deep learning AMIs and custom Ubuntu AMIs with pre-installation scripts. This allows for tailored build environments.

```yaml
# .github/runs-on.yml

# Custom AMI images
images:
  # GPU-enabled deep learning AMI
  dlami-x64:
    platform: "linux"
    arch: "x64"
    owner: "898082745236"
    name: "Deep Learning Base OSS Nvidia Driver GPU AMI (Ubuntu 22.04)*"

  # Custom Ubuntu AMI with preinstall script
  custom-ubuntu:
    owner: "099720109477"
    name: "ubuntu/images/hvm-ssd/ubuntu-jammy-22.04-amd64-server-*"
    arch: x64
    platform: linux
    preinstall: |
      #!/bin/bash
      apt-get update
      apt-get install -y docker.io kubectl
      touch /tmp/preinstalled
```

--------------------------------

### Job Label System - Matrix Builds with Grouping

Source: https://context7.com/runs-on/runs-on/llms.txt

Configures matrix builds that are grouped deterministically using the GitHub run ID. It specifies runner resources and environment for each matrix combination.

```yaml
jobs:
  # Matrix builds with grouping
  matrix:
    strategy:
      matrix:
        version: [1, 2, 3]
    runs-on: runs-on=${{ github.run_id }},cpu=4,ram=8,env=production
```
